mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with trade-offs in complexity and resource requirements. This installation guide provides a straightforward method to deploy vLLM on Kubernetes.

steps.title: Install the vLLM CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM command-line interface, which is necessary for managing vLLM deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.19.1

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in a designated region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install vLLM Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM Helm chart into the Kubernetes cluster, deploying the necessary resources.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.1

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.1

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM with the specified version.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment from the Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the vLLM Helm release.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any critical data. This process includes draining nodes, upgrading the Helm chart, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.1

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Drain the node before upgrade.
> Upgrade to the latest version of vLLM.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application inside the pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource limits or configurations.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the requested pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or adjust the resource requests in the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not have been created or is in a failed state.

**Fix:**
Check the service status with 'kubectl get services -n vllm' and redeploy if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new quantization methods and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older quantization flags in favor of new unified flags.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your configuration files to use the new quantization flags.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n vllm' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
